source: tests/load/fan100.js (k6 v2.0.0) — fan-out of 100 full load lifecycles

issues.title: Accept-offer 500: missing LoadLead_Verifications / Org tables in dev (and on first-boot)
issues.context: tests/load/fan100.js fan-out of 100 lifecycles produced 0 acceptances. Every POST /api/driver/offers/:loadId/accept returned 500 with 'ResourceNotFoundException: Cannot do operations on a non-existent table'. services/carrierOfRecord.ts reads DYNAMODB_VERIFICATIONS_TABLE (and OrgMemberships) but backend/scripts/createTables.mjs only provisions Users/Drivers/Shippers/Receivers/Loads/Offers. Entire post-broadcast lifecycle is unreachable.
issues.user_story: As a driver, I need the accept-offer endpoint to resolve carrier-of-record reliably so that I can book loads end-to-end, including on freshly-provisioned environments.
gherkin: Given a freshly-provisioned environment after running createTables.mjs
And a seeded verified driver with an OFFERED offer
When the driver POSTs /api/driver/offers/:loadId/accept
Then the response status is 200
And the underlying carrier-of-record + verification lookups succeed without ResourceNotFoundException
issues.recommendation: Extend backend/scripts/createTables.mjs to provision LoadLead_Verifications, LoadLead_Organisations, LoadLead_OrgMemberships, LoadLead_OrgInvitations. Add a boot-time existence check that fails loud if any referenced table is missing.

issues.title: Security pass-rate 88.3% on negative /api/admin/orgs probes (target 100%)
issues.context: Of 47 negative probes (driver token -> GET /api/admin/orgs), only 36 returned 403; 11 returned non-403. Direct curl probes return 403 cleanly, so the failures were likely 500s during concurrency (FIND-007). Until FIND-001 + FIND-007 are fixed and the run is reproducible with 100% security pass, this remains a blocker.
issues.user_story: As a security stakeholder, I need every negative authorization probe to return 403 (or 401) under concurrency so we can prove RBAC holds without exception.
gherkin: Given a DRIVER bearer token
When the harness performs 100+ concurrent GET /api/admin/orgs requests
Then 100% of responses are 403 (or 401 for expired)
And zero responses are 200 or 5xx
issues.recommendation: Re-run fan-100 after LOAD-E2E-001 + LOAD-E2E-007. If security_check_pass < 100%, audit the requireAdmin middleware path.

issues.title: Submit drops 28% under 6-VU concurrency
issues.context: 100 drafts succeeded but only 72 submits. Direct probe works. Suspect concurrency on the broadcast/matching service, an idempotency conflict, or a hidden per-shipper rate limit.
issues.user_story: As a shipper, I need submit to succeed under realistic dispatch concurrency, not silently drop 28% of submissions.
gherkin: Given 100 valid drafts created by 2 shippers under 6 concurrent VUs
When each draft is submitted via POST /api/shipper/loads/:loadId/submit
Then at least 95% of submits return 200
And dropped submits emit a structured error the client can retry
issues.recommendation: Instrument the submit handler (counters around broadcast init), add retry-with-backoff on the client, and re-run with 6 VUs.

issues.title: Driver IN_TRANSIT status endpoint missing
issues.context: Spec calls for a 6-stage lifecycle (dispatched -> at pickup -> picked up -> in transit -> at delivery -> delivered). Implementation collapses to BOOKED (on accept) -> DELIVERED (on POD). No driver-facing status transition between them. Probe POST /api/driver/loads/:loadId/status returns 404.
issues.user_story: As a driver, I need to record in-transit milestones (picked up, in transit, at delivery) so shippers and receivers can track progress in real time.
gherkin: Given a driver with an accepted load
When the driver POSTs /api/driver/loads/:loadId/status with status=IN_TRANSIT
Then the response status is 200
And the load's status field reflects the new state
And invalid transitions return 400
issues.recommendation: Add POST /api/driver/loads/:loadId/status with a state-machine validator.

issues.title: Receiver confirm-delivery endpoint missing
issues.context: Spec calls for receiver-side delivery confirmation. Today the driver's POD post auto-marks DELIVERED. Receiver has only read-only /api/receiver/incoming. Probe POST /api/receiver/loads/:loadId/confirm returns 404.
issues.user_story: As a receiver, I need to confirm receipt of a load so we have a chain-of-custody record independent of the driver's POD claim.
gherkin: Given a load that the driver has marked DELIVERED via POD
When the receiver POSTs /api/receiver/loads/:loadId/confirm
Then the response status is 200
And the load record reflects the receiver's confirmation timestamp + user
issues.recommendation: Add POST /api/receiver/loads/:loadId/confirm with receiver authZ check.

issues.title: Matching/broadcast is async with no readiness signal
issues.context: Drivers had to poll loadboard up to 8x over 1.6s before the matched offer appeared. No event or webhook signals broadcast completion. Makes E2E tests racy and produces a poor UX on fresh post.
issues.user_story: As a shipper, when I submit a load I want a deterministic signal that broadcast/matching has completed so my UI can show 'N drivers offered'.
gherkin: Given a shipper submits a load
When the submit POST returns 200
Then the response either includes broadcast-completed=true
Or the shipper can poll GET /api/shipper/loads/:loadId/offers and see a settled count
issues.recommendation: Either make broadcast synchronous on submit (likely OK at our load) or expose GET /api/shipper/loads/:loadId/offers.

issues.title: http_req_failed 36% under 6-VU concurrency
issues.context: Over a third of HTTP calls during the 100-iter run failed (timeouts / non-2xx). Most were the 31 accept-500s + 28 dropped submits + 11 non-403 admin probes. Dev backend appears unstable under modest concurrency.
issues.user_story: As a platform operator, I need the API to remain healthy under at least 6 concurrent VUs in dev, so E2E and load runs produce signal-bearing data.
gherkin: Given the dev backend running with all required DDB tables
When 6 VUs drive a 100-iter fan-out
Then http_req_failed rate stays < 5%
And no 5xx errors propagate to clients
issues.recommendation: Add structured error logging on submit + accept; try npm start against dist/ instead of ts-node-dev for the next run.

issues.title: Auth rate limiter not env-tunable
issues.context: Hard-coded 15-req/15-min limit blocked E2E harness setup. Required adding a dev-only skip() bypass to backend/src/index.ts to unblock 13+ actor logins in setup().
issues.user_story: As a test engineer, I need to tune the auth rate limiter via env in dev/staging without code changes, while keeping production defaults strict.
gherkin: Given RATE_LIMIT_MAX=1000 and RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS=900000 in dev
When E2E setup performs 13 logins in 200ms
Then no request returns 429
And production deploys (APP_ENV=production) ignore the env and keep 15/15min
issues.recommendation: Expose RATE_LIMIT_MAX + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS envs, keep current defaults.
